summary.intent: Address 5 P2 findings from Codex's round-3 review of PR #696 on the audit-agnostic-navigation-components branch, covering lazy loading, rename handling, and steps completion bugs.
summary.outcome: All 5 findings fixed with TDD tests, 569 navigation tests passing, clippy clean, xci-fast all 10 steps passed, committed and pushed to the PR branch.

repo: The ars-ui project uses a custom `xci-fast` CI command with 10 steps as the final gate before pushing.
Codex posts incremental review threads (emoji reactions like 👀) before finishing its full review pass — threads can appear before the 👀 reaction drops.
The spec files at spec/components/navigation/ must be kept in sync with implementation changes and are validated via `cargo xtask spec validate`.

Steps component uses `normalize_current_status` to enforce invariants, but this function is called from both SyncProps and navigation paths — fixes must be scoped carefully to avoid breaking navigation semantics.
TreeView's `ctx.selection_state.disabled_keys` is not automatically recomputed when lazy children are loaded — it must be explicitly rebuilt in the `ChildrenLoaded` handler.
The `ctx.expanded` runtime binding is separate from `TreeItemConfig::default_expanded` in `TreeCollection` — loaded subtrees need their default_expanded merged into ctx.expanded manually.

code.finding: ChildrenLoaded handler needed two fixes: recompute disabled_keys from the new collection, and call collect_default_expanded helper to merge default_expanded configs into ctx.expanded.
on_rename_input_blur API gate relaxed from requiring renaming_key == specific key to just renaming_key.is_some(); RenameCommit transition gate now only rejects when no rename is active at all.
ToggleNode on an Error-state branch now retries load (stays expanded, fires LoadChildren) instead of collapsing.
SyncProps preserve-path must detect statuses[target] == Complete before normalizing and re-apply Complete after — fix scoped to SyncProps only, not normalize_current_status globally.

workflow: Running xci-fast in background while prepping the commit message is an efficient parallel workflow.
When a fix to a shared helper is too broad (breaks other tests), scope the fix to the specific callsite rather than changing the helper's semantics.
Clippy's `use ? operator` suggestion was caught and applied before the final CI gate — running clippy early avoids late-stage blockers.
The poll loop monitoring Codex's 👀 reaction was strictly waiting for reaction drop, causing a delay — jumping ahead to fetch and process threads directly when they're visible is faster.

friction: The background poll loop was waiting for Codex's 👀 reaction to drop before fetching threads, but Codex posts incrementally — 5 threads were already available but not being processed.

Initial R3-T4 Steps fix was too broad (changed normalize_current_status globally), causing a regression in navigation-back-to-completed-step semantics — required a second pass to narrow the fix to the SyncProps callsite only.
Clippy caught a missing `?` operator usage that required an additional edit pass after the initial implementation.
open_items: xci-fast was run in background and completed successfully — no remaining CI concerns, but Codex may post a round-4 review after seeing the push.
The commit references issue #223 alongside #696 — unclear if #223 has separate remaining work beyond what was addressed here.
Resolving and re-triggering Codex on PR #696 was mentioned as the next step after push but not shown as completed in the transcript.
